seoTitle: How to Put Two Pictures Side by Side With Canva
metaDescription: Create a clean side-by-side image comparison or collage in Canva, perfect for promotional banners, before-and-after posts, and product comparisons.
intro: Placing two photos side by side is a simple way to compare products, show a before-and-after, or build a collage for social media and marketing. This guide walks through arranging two images cleanly in Canva, from initial layout to exporting a finished graphic.

steps.heading: Choose a layout or blank canvas
steps.body: Start with a Canva template designed for side-by-side images or set up a blank canvas sized for where the graphic will be used.

steps.heading: Upload and place your two images
steps.body: Add both photos to the canvas and position them next to each other, using grid or frame elements to keep the layout even.

steps.heading: Adjust sizing and spacing
steps.body: Resize each image so they match in scale and add consistent spacing between them for a clean, professional look.

steps.heading: Customize with text or labels
steps.body: Add captions, labels, or a divider if you're comparing products or showing a before-and-after to make the comparison clear.

steps.heading: Export your finished side-by-side image
steps.body: Once the layout looks right, download the graphic in a format suited to social media, presentations, or promotional materials.

faq.question: Can I add more than two pictures side by side in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, the same technique extends to multiple images by adding more frames or grid sections to your layout.

faq.question: Does Canva have templates for before-and-after comparisons?
faq.answer: Canva offers grid and comparison-style templates that work well for before-and-after or side-by-side product images.

faq.question: Is this suitable for small business marketing?
faq.answer: Yes, side-by-side layouts are commonly used for product comparisons, promotional banners, and social media posts by small businesses.

faq.question: Do the two images need to be the same size?
faq.answer: They don't have to start the same size — you can resize and crop each one within Canva to match the layout you want.
